topic: Meta-prediction: CP > 27% for US negative GDP question
summary: CP is right at 27-28% threshold. Average 0.2739. 92% of recent readings at or above 27%. Slight upward trend. Large forecaster base (778) = stability.
content:
Key analysis:
- CP has been at 27-28% (rounded) for ~2 weeks, very stable
- Average precise CP last week: 0.2739 (above 0.27 threshold)
- 92% of readings in last 2 weeks at or above 27%
- Only 8% below 27%
- Transitions from 0.27: 67% stay same, 25% go up, 8% go down
- 778 forecasters = very stable base
- Recent slight upward trend (28% most common in last few days)
- Tariff risks support higher recession probability
- Strong GDP data (Q3 2025 +4.4%, GDPNow Q4 2025 +4.2%) partially offsets

Main risk: economic data releases (jobs report ~Feb 6, CPI ~Feb 12) could shift CP in either direction. But 778 forecasters makes large swings unlikely.

Forecast: ~62% YES (CP will be above 27% on Feb 14)
